summary:
Single-valve replacement is not considered safe in patients with double prostheses because there is insufficient evidence supporting its effectiveness and it carries significant risks of untreated infection dissemination or recurrence. Current guidelines emphasize comprehensive surgical approaches—such as reconstructions or multi-valve replacements—in these complex cases to ensure adequate bacterial clearance and prevent further damage.  

Note: This summary focuses solely on clinically relevant points related to patient outcomes, procedural appropriateness, and underlying pathophysiology within the context of prosthetic valve endocarditis.
